rescue_reasoning: Climate screen is explicit_self, so retain despite missing outcome token. The text explicitly commits to 'hosting three zero-waste markets... by 2030' and 'conducting awareness-raising campaigns' to encourage 'bulk' products, which are direct waste-prevention/resource-efficiency interventions (reduced packaging and waste generation). Primary category Resource Efficiency and mechanism waste_generation_reduction are consistent with the described actions.
policy_statement: Host three zero-waste markets by 2030 and run awareness campaigns to encourage bulk and organic products in markets.
mechanism_description: The city commits to hosting zero-waste markets and running awareness-raising campaigns to encourage bulk and organic products, reducing single-use packaging and overall waste generated.
primary_causal_pathway: Same service/consumption → ↓ material (and packaging) demand → ↓ upstream production + ↓ waste treatment → ↓ GHG emissions
causal_mechanism_detail:
1) Change in human activity/system: Residents and vendors shift purchasing and selling practices (bulk buying, reusable/returnable containers, reduced single-use packaging) prompted by city-hosted zero-waste markets and public awareness campaigns.  
2) Mediating physical/industrial process: Reduced demand for packaging/materials lowers extraction, manufacturing, and transport activity; reduced waste generation lowers collection, processing, incineration, and landfill burdens (including methane formation where landfilling is used).  
3) Climate-relevant outcome: Net reduction in life-cycle GHG emissions associated with materials/packaging and waste management, due to source reduction.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is reduced life-cycle GHG emissions by preventing material and packaging production (and subsequent waste treatment) through lower waste generation.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: Waste prevention reduces material throughput, which most strongly reduces upstream manufacturing and supply-chain emissions (and secondarily waste-treatment emissions).  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: While outcomes include mitigation, the mechanism operates chiefly by using fewer materials for the same retail/food service—i.e., efficiency/source reduction—so Resource Efficiency best captures the dominant lever.  
3) Edge cases applied: If campaigns primarily increase composting/recycling (diversion) rather than prevention, the dominant pathway could shift toward mitigation via landfill methane avoidance; however, this canonical mechanism is explicitly “waste generation reduction” via zero-waste purchasing/packaging avoidance.  
4) Applicability to ALL instances: Any policy fitting this mechanism (markets/campaigns that reduce single-use packaging and total waste generated) works through reduced material demand and reduced waste handling, making Resource Efficiency the consistent primary category.  
5) Secondary subtype deferred: Whether the emissions reductions come more from avoided packaging production vs avoided landfill/incineration depends on local waste composition and treatment; assign secondary attributes only with policy- and context-specific evidence.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: Could be classified as Mitigation if the mechanism were framed/implemented mainly as diverting organics from landfill (methane avoidance) rather than preventing waste generation; ensure the policy evidence shows source reduction (less packaging/material use) as the core action.
key_indicators: "committed to hosting three zero-waste markets"; "by 2030"; "conducting awareness-raising campaigns"; "encourage the development of 'bulk' and 'organic' products"
co_benefits: Reduced local litter and waste management costs; improved environmental awareness/education; potential health benefits from increased organic consumption.
instance_edge_case_notes: Could also be coded as a combined instrument (programmatic demonstration + information campaign). Chosen as “incentive-program” because the city is providing/organizing a voluntary participation venue (zero-waste markets) alongside outreach rather than setting binding rules or standards.
